atoms.content: A derivative measures the rate at which a function's output changes as its input changes. In ML, it helps us know how to adjust parameters to reduce errors.
Imagine driving a car. Your position is the function. The derivative of your position is your speed (how fast your position is changing at any exact moment).

1. Slope of the tangent line at a point.
2. Denoted as dy/dx or f'(x).
3. Zero derivative means a peak or valley (minimum error!).
A partial derivative measures how a function with multiple variables changes when we adjust just ONE variable, keeping all other variables completely frozen.
Imagine baking a cake. How does changing the amount of sugar affect sweetness, assuming flour and eggs stay exactly the same? That's a partial derivative.
If you are finding the minimum of a curve, what should the derivative be at that lowest point? (Answer: Zero)
